name: MT-STGAT: Multi-Task Spatiotemporal Graph Attention Network
description: M.E. major research project for intelligent power-grid fault detection, classification, and localization using GRU + GATv2 on the IEEE 118-bus grid.
readmeSummary: Designed a GRU + GATv2 multi-task spatiotemporal graph attention architecture for fault detection, classification, and localization on the IEEE 118-bus grid. Achieved 100% fault detection accuracy, 81.29% macro-F1 score, and 66.94% Top-1 localization accuracy. IEEE conference paper submitted; repository/link available to recruiters on request until publication.
title: MT-STGAT: Multi-Task Spatiotemporal Graph Attention Network
longDesc: M.E. Major Research Project at Chandigarh University, 2025-2026. IEEE conference paper submitted. Built a research-level GRU + GATv2 architecture for fault detection, classification, and localization on the IEEE 118-bus power grid.
keyFeatures: Private/hidden repository available to recruiters on request until publication
recruiterSummary: Research-level M.E. major project applying GRU and GATv2 graph attention modeling to power-grid fault detection, classification, and localization. The work demonstrates advanced AI research ability, graph neural network design, rigorous evaluation, and domain application in smart grids. GitHub/repository access can be provided to recruiters on request until IEEE publication is complete.
problemSolved: Improves intelligent monitoring of electrical power grids by detecting, classifying, and localizing faults on a complex IEEE 118-bus benchmark system.
projectType: M.E. Major Research Project, IEEE Paper Submitted, Private Repository
learningOutcomes: Designing multi-task graph neural network architectures
Applying spatiotemporal deep learning to power systems
Evaluating detection, classification, and localization metrics
Preparing research work for IEEE conference submission

name: Single-Lead ECG Diagnostic Analysis using Deep Learning
description: M.Tech/M.E. major research group project evaluating deep learning models for cardiac disease classification from ECG signals using the PTB-XL dataset.
readmeSummary: Trained and evaluated 48 deep learning models including ResNet1D, InceptionTime, and BiLSTM on the PTB-XL dataset with 21,799 ECG records across 5 cardiac disease classes. Conducted greedy lead reduction experiments and identified a non-linear performance cliff below 4 leads. Group major research project; repository/link available to recruiters on request until publication.
title: Single-Lead ECG Diagnostic Analysis using Deep Learning
longDesc: M.Tech/M.E. Major Research Group Project, 2025-2026. Trained and evaluated 48 deep learning models on PTB-XL ECG records for cardiac disease classification and lead-reduction analysis.
keyFeatures: 48 trained and evaluated deep learning models
Observed non-linear performance cliff below 4 leads
Private/hidden repository available to recruiters on request until publication
recruiterSummary: Research-level healthcare AI group project focused on ECG diagnostic analysis. Benchmarked 48 deep learning configurations on PTB-XL, covering ResNet1D, InceptionTime, and BiLSTM for 5 cardiac disease classes, with additional lead-reduction experiments. Repository access can be provided to recruiters on request until publication is complete.
problemSolved: Explores how accurately cardiac disease classes can be detected from reduced ECG lead configurations, helping evaluate practical low-lead diagnostic AI systems.
industryUseCase: Healthcare, Clinical Decision Support, Biomedical AI
projectType: M.Tech/M.E. Major Research Group Project, Private Repository
learningOutcomes: Benchmarking deep learning models for biomedical time-series data
Working with large ECG datasets
Designing lead-reduction experiments
Analyzing performance tradeoffs in diagnostic AI systems
